message.content:
You are verifier #2, independent. Do NOT optimistically confirm. Read the ACTUAL code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay and independently determine whether this is a TRUE production-readiness problem in the CURRENT code (3 hardening passes already applied).

Finding G-011: discover_and_confirm does not type-guard the confirmer's return value (asymmetric with adjudication/pipeline)
File: src/assay_engine/methodology/discovery.py:150
Description: In adjudicate_with_baseline (adjudication.py:253) and the pipeline runner (pipeline.py:563) a confirmer that forgets to return (yields None) is caught with an explicit isinstance(verdict, Verdict) guard (#F-021). The standalone discovery runner was left out: after verdict = confirm(hypothesis, held_out) (discovery.py:150) it goes straight to verdict.hypothesis_id (:151, which only checks id-mismatch, not type). A confirmer returning None raises an opaque AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'hypothesis_id' instead of the documented typed FirewallViolation. The module docstring (lines 94-97) promises FirewallViolation on a verdict<->hypothesis misattribution, and a None verdict never reaches that check, breaking the typed-exception contract for one of the two public Firewall-B entry points.
PoC: def bad_confirm(hyp, held_out): pass  # returns None. discover_and_confirm(corpus, split, discover=..., confirm=bad_confirm, authority=...) raises AttributeError("'NoneType' object has no attribute 'hypothesis_id'") instead of FirewallViolation('confirm returned NoneType ... expected a Verdict').
Suggested fix: Mirror the adjudication/pipeline guard immediately after confirm(): if not isinstance(verdict, Verdict): raise FirewallViolation(f"confirm returned {type(verdict).__name__} for hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} — expected a Verdict"). Verdict is already imported (line 39).

Confirmed=true ONLY if the problem exists as described in current code, the PoC is reproducible, and it is a genuine concern (not speculative, not already-handled, not intended design). Be concrete and cite file:line.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

90	    adapter); the engine ships no silent-accept default. A study's ``discover`` step locks each
91	    discovered hypothesis (e.g. via :func:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.
92	    lock_hypothesis`) before returning it.
93	
94	    Returns the verdicts. Raises ``FirewallViolation`` (incl. ``PreRegistrationError``) on an
95	    empty discovery or held-out partition (split ids that select no corpus units — a vacuous
96	    test), a non-``DISCOVERY`` hypothesis, a hypothesis that is not verifiably pre-registered
97	    before confirmation, or a verdict↔hypothesis misattribution.
98	    """
99	    discovery_corpus = subset_corpus(corpus, split.discovery_ids)
100	    if not discovery_corpus.units:
101	        # the discovery partition selects no actual corpus units — a hypothesis "discovered"
102	        # from zero data is not data-surfaced at all; it would confirm cleanly on the held-out
103	        # set and silently launder a hand-authored claim through Firewall B. Fail loud.
104	        raise FirewallViolation(
105	            "discovery partition selects no corpus units — nothing to discover from "
106	            "(do split ids match the corpus unit ids?)"
107	        )
108	    hypotheses = list(discover(discovery_corpus))  # sees only the discovery partition
109	    if not hypotheses:
110	        # discovery surfaced nothing — a discovery run with zero hypotheses produces no
111	        # confirmatory verdict at all and is indistinguishable from a silently broken
112	        # ``discover`` callable that forgot to return. Fail loud rather than complete vacuously
113	        # (pass 3, #F-042; mirrors the empty-partition guards above).
114	        raise FirewallViolation(
115	            "discover() returned no hypotheses — a discovery run with zero hypotheses is "
116	            "vacuous and cannot produce a confirmatory verdict"
117	        )
118	
119	    held_out = subset_corpus(corpus, split.confirm_ids)  # disjoint from discovery (split invariant)
120	    if not held_out.units:
121	        # the confirmation partition selects no actual corpus units — confirming on an empty
122	        # held-out set is a vacuous test (the split ids don't match the corpus). Fail loud.
123	        raise FirewallViolation(
124	            "confirmation partition selects no corpus units — nothing to confirm on "
125	            "(do split ids match the corpus unit ids?)"
126	        )
127	    verdicts: list[Verdict] = []
128	    # within-run hypothesis uniqueness (pass 3, #F-018): a ``discover`` step returning the same
129	    # hypothesis_id twice would append two verdicts for one hypothesis, inflating the verdict
130	    # count. The pipeline runner guards this via ``_require_unique_ids``; mirror it in the
131	    # public standalone runner so both entry points enforce the same identity invariant.
132	    seen_hypothesis_ids: set[str] = set()
133	    for hypothesis in hypotheses:
134	        if hypothesis.hypothesis_id in seen_hypothesis_ids:
135	            raise FirewallViolation(
136	                f"duplicate hypothesis_id {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} returned by discover() — "
137	                "each discovered hypothesis must have a unique id"
138	            )
139	        seen_hypothesis_ids.add(hypothesis.hypothesis_id)
140	        if hypothesis.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY:
141	            raise FirewallViolation(
142	                f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is {hypothesis.origin.value!r}; the "
143	                "discovery runner expects DISCOVERY-origin (data-surfaced) hypotheses"
144	            )
145	        # Pre-registration enforced by the runner: the proof must bind THIS hypothesis's content
146	        # (no post-lock content swap) and its attested lock time must precede this confirmation.
147	        require_preregistered(
148	            hypothesis, authority=authority, not_after=_dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc)
149	        )
150	        verdict = confirm(hypothesis, held_out)
151	        if verdict.hypothesis_id != hypothesis.hypothesis_id:
152	            raise FirewallViolation(
153	                f"verdict reports hypothesis_id {verdict.hypothesis_id!r} for hypothesis "
154	                f"{hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
155	            )
156	        verdicts.append(verdict)
157	    return verdicts
158

245	        # Pre-registration: the proof must bind THIS hypothesis's content (no post-lock content
246	        # swap) and its attested lock time must precede the baseline build (so the claim-derived
247	        # hypothesis cannot have been tuned to the baseline).
248	        require_preregistered(hypothesis, authority=authority, not_after=not_after)
249	        verdict = confirm(hypothesis, baseline, claim)
250	        # A confirmer that forgets to `return` yields None; accessing verdict.hypothesis_id
251	        # would raise an opaque AttributeError instead of a typed firewall error (pass 3,
252	        # #F-021). Guard the contract explicitly.
253	        if not isinstance(verdict, Verdict):
254	            raise FirewallViolation(
255	                f"confirm returned {type(verdict).__name__} for hypothesis "
256	                f"{hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} — expected a Verdict"
257	            )
258	        if verdict.hypothesis_id != hypothesis.hypothesis_id:

261	                f"{verdict.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
262	            )
263	        verdicts.append(verdict)
264	        if on_step is not None:
265	            # per-claim hook (in order) so a caller can record the pre-registered hypothesis AND
266	            # its verdict to provenance as each happens, not in a post-hoc batch (#93).
267	            on_step(hypothesis, verdict)

content.text:
Rather than build a full valid corpus + preregistration proof (which require_preregistered would gate), I can directly confirm the code path. The reproduction is structurally clear from the code: line 150 `verdict = confirm(...)` followed immediately by line 151 `verdict.hypothesis_id` with no type guard. A `confirm` returning `None` will hit `None.hypothesis_id` → `AttributeError`.

Let me write a minimal unit-style reproduction that isolates the confirm-return path, bypassing the preregistration gate by patching, to definitively prove the AttributeError.

content.text: Now let me build the full PoC. I'll bypass the preregistration gate by monkeypatching `require_preregistered` to a no-op so we reach line 150 (the gate is upstream and not what's being tested).

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && .venv/bin/python -c "
import assay_engine.methodology.discovery as disc
from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import DiscoverConfirmSplit, FirewallViolation
from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisOrigin, HypothesisKind
from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus, Unit

# no-op the preregistration gate (upstream of the seam under test)
disc.require_preregistered = lambda *a, **k: None

corpus = Corpus(units=(Unit(unit_id='d1', text='x'), Unit(unit_id='c1', text='y')), relations=(), metadata={})
split = DiscoverConfirmSplit(discovery_ids=frozenset({'d1'}), confirm_ids=frozenset({'c1'}))

def discover(c):
    return [Hypothesis(hypothesis_id='h1', statement='s', kind=HypothesisKind.UNIT_LEVEL,
                       origin=HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY, test_name='t', decision_rule='r')]

def bad_confirm(hyp, held_out):
    pass  # returns None

try:
    disc.discover_and_confirm(corpus, split, discover=discover, confirm=bad_confirm, authority=None)
    print('NO ERROR (unexpected)')
except FirewallViolation as e:
    print('FirewallViolation (typed contract held):', e)
except AttributeError as e:
    print('AttributeError (contract BROKEN):', e)
" 2>&1 | tail -5
input.description: Run the PoC for None-returning confirmer

content.content: AttributeError (contract BROKEN): 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'hypothesis_id'

content.text:
Reproduced exactly as described. The PoC raises `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'hypothesis_id'` instead of the documented typed `FirewallViolation`.

All claims in the finding are verified:

1. **Asymmetry is real**: `adjudication.py:253` and `pipeline.py:563` both have `if not isinstance(verdict, Verdict): raise FirewallViolation(...)`. `discovery.py` has no such guard.
2. **discovery.py:150-151**: `verdict = confirm(hypothesis, held_out)` is immediately followed by `if verdict.hypothesis_id != hypothesis.hypothesis_id:` — only an id-mismatch check, no type guard.
3. **Docstring contract** (discovery.py:94-97): promises `FirewallViolation` on a "verdict↔hypothesis misattribution"; a `None` verdict never reaches that typed check.
4. **PoC reproduced**: `AttributeError("'NoneType' object has no attribute 'hypothesis_id'")` — exactly the opaque error described, not the typed `FirewallViolation`.
5. **`Verdict` is imported** at discovery.py:39, so the suggested fix is directly applicable.
6. **Public entry point**: `discover_and_confirm` is exported (methodology/__init__.py:22,61), one of two public Firewall-B runners.

The only inaccuracy in the finding is the path: pipeline.py is at `src/assay_engine/pipeline.py` (line 563 correct), not `src/assay_engine/methodology/pipeline.py`. This does not affect the substance.

Assessment: This is a genuine but minor robustness/contract-consistency defect. It is a divergence from a contract the codebase explicitly established and enforced in two sibling code paths (the #F-021 pattern from pass 3). Impact is low: it only manifests when a study author writes a buggy `confirm` callable that forgets to return; the failure is still loud (an exception), just an untyped/opaque one rather than the documented `FirewallViolation`. No data corruption, no silent firewall bypass. Severity: low.

input.reasoning:
Verified against current code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. The asymmetry is real and the PoC reproduces exactly.

Evidence:
- discovery.py:150 `verdict = confirm(hypothesis, held_out)` is followed immediately by discovery.py:151 `if verdict.hypothesis_id != hypothesis.hypothesis_id:` — an id-mismatch check only, with NO type guard.
- adjudication.py:253 has `if not isinstance(verdict, Verdict): raise FirewallViolation(...)` (the #F-021 pattern), and the pipeline runner has the same guard at src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:563. discovery.py is the only one of the three confirm-seam call sites lacking it (grep for `isinstance(verdict, Verdict)` returns only adjudication.py).
- The module docstring (discovery.py:94-97) promises FirewallViolation on a verdict<->hypothesis misattribution; a None verdict never reaches that typed check.
- `Verdict` is imported at discovery.py:39, so the suggested fix is directly applicable.
- discover_and_confirm is a public export (methodology/__init__.py:22,61), one of the two public Firewall-B runners.

PoC reproduced (with the upstream require_preregistered gate stubbed to a no-op, since it is not the seam under test): a `confirm` returning None raises `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'hypothesis_id'` instead of the documented typed FirewallViolation. Confirmed via the project venv (.venv/bin/python; system python3 is 3.9 and cannot load slots= dataclasses).

Minor finding-text inaccuracy: pipeline.py lives at src/assay_engine/pipeline.py, not src/assay_engine/methodology/pipeline.py; line 563 is correct. Does not affect substance.

Severity: low. The defect is a real contract-consistency divergence from an invariant the codebase explicitly established and enforced in two sibling paths, but impact is limited: it only triggers when a study author writes a buggy confirm callable that forgets to return, and the failure is still loud (an exception is raised) — just untyped/opaque rather than the documented FirewallViolation. No silent firewall bypass, no data corruption.

content.text: Confirmed finding G-011 as a true (low-severity) defect. The type-guard asymmetry is real, the PoC reproduces the `AttributeError` instead of the documented `FirewallViolation`, and the fix is directly applicable since `Verdict` is already imported at discovery.py:39.
